title: excoecaria sylvatica
intro: Excoecaria sylvatica, also known as the poison tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to tropical regions of the Pacific Islands and Southeast Asia. The plant contains a toxic compound called phorbol, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including skin conditions, fever, and rheumatism. Excoecaria sylvatica has been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain, inflammation, and the risk of infection. The plant is traditionally prepared as a paste or decoction, and has been used in herbal remedies to treat a range of health issues, including wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems.
